feature: Nautilus v2 — RKM Foundation + P0 attestation primitives

tasks.title: Extend AuditEntry.event_type Literal + schema_version field
tasks.description: Widen AuditEntry.event_type Literal at nautilus/core/models.py:218 from 2 to 21 values (attestation_emitted, session_token_issued, session_token_verification_failed, signing_key_rotated, signing_key_revoked, proposal_emitted, proposal_validated, proposal_approved, proposal_rejected, proposal_promoted, meta_rule_fired, relationship_observed, rule_promoted, rule_retracted, rule_rolled_back, adapter_quarantined, adapter_unquarantined, schema_drift_detected, schema_drift_severity_overridden). Add schema_version: int = None field per AC-A.1 backwards-compat pattern. Extend tests/integration/test_backwards_compat.py:66 to round-trip Phase-1 fixture.
tasks.notes: Additive only; v1 readers ignore new event_types and schema_version field. DQ5 requires drift-guard test pairing Literal with nautilus events list enumeration.

tasks.title: Add Adapter.get_schema() Protocol method (both in-repo and SDK)
tasks.description: Add async def get_schema(self) -> AdapterSchema method to Adapter Protocol at nautilus/adapters/base.py:128 AND synchronously update SDK Protocol at packages/nautilus-adapter-sdk/src/nautilus_adapter_sdk/protocols.py:13. Create nautilus/adapters/schema.py with AdapterField, AdapterTable, AdapterSchema dataclasses (frozen), and classify_drift() helper for severity tiers. Ship new drift-guard test tests/unit/adapters/test_get_schema_drift_guard.py verifying SDK and in-repo Protocol remain synchronized per AC-21.a.
tasks.notes: Embedder Protocol explicitly excluded per OQ1. AC-21.a scopes to 8 data-source adapters only. Drift-guard test is critical AC-21.a sub-requirement.

tasks.title: Add response_hash + hash_skipped to attestation payload builder
tasks.description: Modify nautilus/core/attestation_payload.py:169 build_payload() to accept response_hash: str | None and hash_skipped: bool parameters. Add compute_response_hash(canonical_bytes: bytes) -> str helper function using _sha256() at line 69 (reuse anchor). Extend return dict with 'response_hash' and 'hash_skipped' fields per AC-19.a. Add byte-mutation test fixture in tests/integration/test_response_hash_byte_mutation.py that computes response_hash, mutates one byte of adapter response, verifies hash mismatch per AC-19.f.
tasks.notes: Per-response only in v2.0 per AC-19.d. hash_skipped=true for non-deterministic adapters per AC-19.g (DQ2 locked). Reuses existing _stable_json and _sha256 infrastructure.

tasks.title: Implement SessionTokenService + KeyRing + JWKS export
tasks.description: Create nautilus/attestation/session_token.py with SessionTokenClaims dataclass, SessionTokenService class (issue/verify), and SessionTokenError exception per AC-18.a-g. Create nautilus/attestation/key_ring.py with KeyEntry dataclass and KeyRing class (primary/verifier_for/rotate/revoke/active) using Ed25519 + PyJWT, persisting to .nautilus/keys/*.pem per AC-18.e. Create nautilus/attestation/jwks.py with export_jwks() per RFC 7517 returning dict per AC-18.c. Mount GET /v1/keys/jwks.json endpoint in fastapi_app.py. Mount POST /v1/sessions in fastapi_app.py returning SessionTokenClaims JSON. Add verify_session_token dependency at nautilus/transport/auth.py:71 pattern. Add key rotation CLI stub mount (details in task-027).
tasks.notes: Disjoint from Fathom AttestationService per Risk #5. Uses existing cryptography+PyJWT deps. Key rotation window enables ≥2 active keys per AC-18.e. Audit emission for session_token_issued/verification_failed wired here.

tasks.title: Wire response_hash into Broker._sign() and emit attestation_emitted event
tasks.description: Modify nautilus/core/broker.py:1207 _sign() method to extract response from response_metadata, compute response_hash via nautilus/core/attestation_payload.py compute_response_hash(), pass to build_payload() per AC-19.a. Wire attestation_emitted audit event emission per AC-19.b linkage. Handle hash_skipped=true for non-deterministic adapters per AC-19.g (skip hash computation, sign claim instead).
tasks.notes: Surgical change; only modifies _sign call site. Handles legacy adapters gracefully per AC-19.e (missing hash = null, legacy=true). Replay-protection achieved via asymmetric signing.

tasks.title: Hook SchemaFingerprintStore into adapter registration + implement get_schema() for 8 adapters
tasks.description: Create SchemaFingerprintStore class in nautilus/adapters/schema.py (record fingerprint at registration, compare on broker startup, emit schema_drift_detected per AC-21.e). Hook into nautilus/core/broker.py:393 _build_adapter registry merge point. Implement async get_schema() method for all 8 in-repo adapters (postgres, pgvector, neo4j, elasticsearch, influxdb, rest, servicenow, s3) per OQ3 per-adapter strategy (postgres/pgvector via information_schema, neo4j via schema visualization, elasticsearch via _mapping, etc.). Mount GET /v1/adapters/{name}/schema endpoints returning AdapterSchema JSON per AC-21.a. Implement quarantine logic returning ADAPTER_QUARANTINED envelope when major drift detected per design.md envelope.
tasks.notes: Largest task in Phase B. Splits conceptually as: (a) fingerprint store + broker hook (100 LOC), (b) per-adapter get_schema() impls (450 LOC, ~50 each). Per OQ3 design, each adapter's implementation differs. Fingerprints stored at .nautilus/adapters/fingerprints/*.json per module layout.

tasks.title: Implement ProposalQueue with JSONL + lockf persistence
tasks.description: Create nautilus/rkm/queue.py with ProposalQueue class implementing JSONL append-only queue at .nautilus/rkm/queue/ per DQ3 (fcntl.lockf filesystem lock). Implement list()/get()/append() methods persisting proposal records (schema per design.md lines 145–178). Add lockf contention handling with timeout per AC-35.9.a. Tests verify lockf contention under concurrent writes per task-007 focus.
tasks.notes: Mirrors FileAttestationSink pattern. Stdlib fcntl.lockf only per constraints. No external filelock dep. Types.py defines Proposal, ValidationError, ConfidenceBreakdown dataclasses.

tasks.title: Implement AuditEventEmitter + wire audit emission path for meta-rule firings
tasks.description: Create nautilus/rkm/audit_emitter.py with AuditEventEmitter class providing emit_event(sparse_entry: dict) -> AuditEntry method per OQ1 resolution. Wire into FathomRouter.__init__ (receives emitter as injected dependency). Add AuditLogger.emit_event() method for meta_rule_fired and proposal_emitted events (OQ1 stage-4 resolution: option b, new emit_event method). Emit audit events in correct locations: session_token_issued/verification_failed in SessionTokenService, signing_key_rotated/revoked in KeyRing, attestation_emitted in Broker._sign.
tasks.notes: OQ1 is load-bearing; must resolve before #35.3/#35.4 land. Implementation choice: new emit_event(sparse_entry) method on AuditLogger enables downstream code to emit events without full Broker context. Tests verify meta_rule_fired events propagate correctly.

tasks.title: Create RKM relationship templates + manual facts loader
tasks.description: Create nautilus/rules/templates/rkm.yaml with source_relationship and data_type_affinity CLIPS templates per AC-35.2.a (template syntax, slot definitions). Create nautilus/rules/modules/curator.yaml defining curator module per AC-35.3.a (module registration, not isolated meta-rules — those land in phase-D). Create facts/relationships/ directory with manual relationship YAML loader reading *.yaml files per AC-35.2.b. Implement nautilus/rules/facts.py with load_manual_relationships() function asserting facts into broker environment at startup. Validate slots (relationship_type enum, confidence 0.0–1.0 range) at load time per AC-35.2.e with structured error messages per file:line.
tasks.notes: AC-35.2.c/d (pattern-matching + restart persistence) tested in integration suite. Curator module is container for meta-rules; no rule isolation logic here (that lands in task-010 via isolation.py).

tasks.title: Implement pattern-tracker meta-rules + curator-isolation enforcement
tasks.description: Create nautilus/rules/meta/pattern-tracker.yaml with track-sequential-requests and strengthen-relationship-candidate rules per AC-35.3.b-c (assert relationship_candidate facts, increment observation_count on repeats). Create nautilus/rkm/curator/isolation.py with parse-time static-analysis check preventing meta-rules from modifying routing module facts per AC-35.3.e and OQ2. Implement curator-isolation test in tests/unit/rkm/test_curator_isolation.py verifying cross-module assertion rejection. Wire pattern-tracker into curator module at FathomRouter load time.
tasks.notes: OQ2 (isolation enforcement strategy) resolved via parse-time YAML check. AC-35.3.d meta_rule_fired event emission depends on task-008 AuditEventEmitter plumbing.

tasks.title: Implement auto-promotion meta-rules + feature flag
tasks.description: Create meta-rules promote-high-confidence-relationship and flag-low-confidence-for-human in pattern-tracker ruleset (or separate if >100 LOC). Emit rule proposal records per AC-35.4.a when observation_count > 9 (relationship facts or suggestion-module rules only, routing rules statically unreachable per AC-35.4.a Q1 lock). Add rkm.auto_promote.enabled config flag at config/models.py (default OFF per AC-35.4.d). Implement static-analysis test verifying routing rules cannot be auto-promoted. Wire proposal emission into queue.append() from meta-rule context (OQ1 path).
tasks.notes: Flag default OFF permanent safety gate in v2.0. Static test asserts no routing-module artifact reachable from auto-promote paths. Lineage metadata (originating rule, observation IDs) persisted to proposal record per AC-35.4.c.

tasks.title: Implement static-analysis validator wrapping Fathom.validate()
tasks.description: Create nautilus/rkm/validator/static.py with validate_rule_yaml(rule_yaml_str) -> (bool, list[ValidationError]) wrapping Fathom.validate(). Detect: YAML→CLIPS compilation errors, unknown template references, unknown/mistyped slots, duplicate rule names within module, cross-module fact references not in allowlist per AC-35.5.b. Return structured ValidationError with file:line and remediation hint per AC-35.5.d. Optimize to <500ms per single rule per AC-35.5.c. Wire CLI `nautilus rules validate <file>` subcommand in task-027 (cli/rules.py).
tasks.notes: Wraps existing Fathom.validate(); no new CLIPS tooling. Performance baseline: compile + validate <500ms on laptop. Errors formatted per design.md Validation Pipeline Rejection block.

tasks.title: Implement shadow/subsumption check validator + fixture suite
tasks.description: Create nautilus/rkm/validator/shadow.py with shadow_check(proposed_rule, existing_rules) -> list[(existing_rule, relation)] function detecting subsumption/shadowing/salience-inversion per AC-35.6.a. Create fixture suite under tests/fixtures/rkm/shadow-pairs/ with ≥20 hand-curated rule pairs (rule_a.yaml, rule_b.yaml, expected_relation.txt) per AC-35.6.a. Implement fixture loader in tests/integration/test_rkm_pipeline.py validating against fixture suite (fail if false-negative; false-positives acceptable per AC-35.6.b). Attach shadow_flags to proposal record per AC-35.6.c.
tasks.notes: Test infrastructure budget: ≥20 hand-curated pairs is real work, not free. Conservative heuristic-based implementation acceptable for v2.0. Fixture suite itself ≈200 LOC (YAML pairs).

tasks.title: Implement sandbox replay harness + replay logic
tasks.description: Create nautilus/rkm/validator/sandbox.py with sandbox_replay(proposed_rule, audit_entries: list[AuditEntry], N: int = 1000) -> SandboxResult recording per-request (was_allowed_before, is_allowed_after, fired_this_rule, cascade_depth, wm_size_delta) per AC-35.7.a-b. Reject on any regression (AC-35.7.c); flag on relaxation/dead-rule/memory-growth/>5%/cascade>3 (AC-35.7.d). Run <60s on laptop for N=1000 per AC-35.7.e. Handle insufficient-history when audit < min_entries per AC-35.7.f (default 100, configurable via rkm.sandbox.min_entries). Create tests/integration/test_rkm_sandbox.py with replay harness tests.
tasks.notes: Test infrastructure budget: replay harness doesn't exist today. Closest precedent is tests/integration/test_fathom_smoke.py. Performance target: <60s is achievable with naive per-request CLIPS environment fresh-load; optimize after green.

tasks.title: Implement confidence scoring pure function
tasks.description: Create nautilus/rkm/validator/scoring.py with score_proposal(sandbox_result, shadow_flags, static_result, proposal) -> (float, ConfidenceBreakdown) pure function per AC-35.8.a. Base 1.0; apply penalties per design-doc formula: −0.3/regression, −0.2/relaxation, −0.1/shadow, −0.1 if fire-rate <5%, −0.05/cascade-warn. No I/O. Unit test boundary values 0.6, 0.9 per AC-35.8.b-c. Score >0.9 eligible for auto-promotion (gated by feature flag per AC-35.8.b); 0.6–0.9 human review; <0.6 reject per AC-35.8.d.
tasks.notes: Simplest validator module; pure function with no dependencies beyond dataclass types. ConfidenceBreakdown includes all penalty terms for CLI display.

tasks.title: Implement LineageStore with cycle detection
tasks.description: Create nautilus/rkm/lineage.py with LineageStore class implementing lineage record persistence at .nautilus/rkm/lineage/*.json per AC-35.10.b. Store proposer, observation_id_range, sandbox_results, approver identity, timestamp, derived_from (upstream rule lineage) per AC-35.10.c. Implement cycle-detection on derived_from DAG per AC-35.10.d preventing circular retraction chains. Implement insert(), get(), list_by_derived_from() methods. Persist as JSON one-file-per-rule-version.
tasks.notes: Mirrors QueueStore pattern (persistent, simple JSON serialization). Cycle detection via DFS on derived_from graph.

tasks.title: Implement review queue operations + promotion logic
tasks.description: Create nautilus/rkm/review.py with review-queue operations: approve_proposal(proposal_id, reviewer_identity) → marks approved, emits proposal_approved audit event, promotes rule into FathomRouter, inserts lineage record per AC-35.10.a-b; reject_proposal(proposal_id, reviewer_identity, reason) → marks rejected, emits proposal_rejected. Handle idempotent approve (already_approved returns 409 per design.md) per AC-35.9.e. Implement cascade retraction logic for AC-35.10.d (--cascade vs --orphan-children flags wired in CLI per task-025).
tasks.notes: Ties together queue + lineage + router. Promotion path: queue.approved → review.promote_rule → fathom_router.reload + lineage.insert.

tasks.title: Implement REST endpoints for RKM + rules operations
tasks.description: Mount REST endpoints in nautilus/transport/fastapi_app.py per AC-35.9-35.10: GET /v1/rkm/queue (list proposals), GET /v1/rkm/queue/{id} (show), POST /v1/rkm/queue/{id}/approve (approve with X-Nautilus-Reviewer header), POST /v1/rkm/queue/{id}/reject (reject with reason). GET /v1/rules/{name}/lineage (show lineage tree). POST /v1/rules/{name}/retract (retract with reason + --yes header). POST /v1/rules/{name}/rollback (rollback to version per AC-35.10.d). All endpoints call review.py backing functions.
tasks.notes: Thin HTTP wrappers over review.py. X-Nautilus-Reviewer header per design.md reviewer-identity-flow. All destructive ops require --yes flag enforcement at fastapi level.

tasks.title: Implement Prometheus metrics for RKM queue depth and age
tasks.description: Create Prometheus metrics in nautilus/core/metrics.py or equivalent: nautilus_rkm_queue_depth (gauge, current queue size), nautilus_rkm_queue_oldest_age_seconds (gauge, timestamp delta to oldest proposal). Wire into queue.list() and broker startup per AC-35.9.f. Mount metrics endpoint at fastapi_app.py (reuse existing Prometheus handler if present).
tasks.notes: Simple gauge metrics. No test required per prd.json validation (contracts cover observability).

tasks.title: Split CLI into package structure + common utilities
tasks.description: Delete nautilus/cli.py. Create nautilus/cli/ package with __init__.py (main() + _build_parser orchestrator + dispatch ladder), _common.py (--json/--yes flag handling, NAUTILUS_REVIEWER env var sourcing, isatty detection, OK/WARN/ERROR/FAIL prefix helpers per OQ5). Move existing health.py, version.py, serve.py from top-level into cli/ per OQ4 Harbor template.
tasks.notes: Foundational CLI work; no new features. All subcommand modules (task-021–026) import from _common.py. OQ4 resolution: split into package structure per Harbor template.

tasks.title: Implement RKM CLI surface (queue + lineage commands)
tasks.description: Create nautilus/cli/rkm.py with subcommands: queue list (--status, --min-confidence, --json), queue show <id> (--json), queue approve <id>, queue reject <id> --reason, queue diff <id>, lineage <proposal_id|rule_name> (--depth, --json) per AC-35.9.b and design.md screens §nautilus rkm queue. Wire to REST endpoints from task-018. Use _common.py helpers for output formatting (OK/ERROR/WARN prefixes, --json JSON-lines, --yes validation). Handle error states per design.md (Empty, Loading, Error, Success for list; Not Found, Success for show).
tasks.notes: DQ4: reviewer identity from NAUTILUS_REVIEWER env var (no auto-detect; honor-system per design.md). ~20 LOC per subcommand; 50–150 LOC total for rkm.py group.

tasks.title: Implement rule CLI surface (retract + lineage + rollback)
tasks.description: Create nautilus/cli/rule.py with subcommands: retract <name> --reason --yes (--cascade | --orphan-children), lineage <name> (--depth, --json), history <name> (--json), rollback <name> --to-version --reason --yes per AC-35.10.a-d. Wire to review.py backing via REST endpoints from task-018. Enforce --yes flag for destructive ops; enforce --reason for retract per design.md. Handle cascade vs orphan-children flags per AC-35.10.d.
tasks.notes: Destructive operations; all require --yes + --reason. CLI mirrors REST POST /v1/rules/{name}/retract pattern.

tasks.title: Implement adapters CLI surface (schema + schema-diff + schema-ack)
tasks.description: Create nautilus/cli/adapters.py with subcommands: list (--status, --json), schema <name> (--json), schema-fingerprint <name>, schema-diff <name>, schema-ack <name> --reason --yes per AC-21.f-g. Wire to REST endpoints + SchemaFingerprintStore from task-006. Show current schema, drift alerts, fingerprint hash. `schema-ack` records reviewer + timestamp, updates fingerprint via POST /v1/adapters/{name}/schema-ack per AC-21.g.
tasks.notes: Extends existing `nautilus adapters` subcommand group. schema-ack is destructive; requires --yes.

tasks.title: Implement key CLI surface (list + rotate + revoke)
tasks.description: Create nautilus/cli/key.py with subcommands: list (--json), rotate (--remove-old, --yes), revoke <kid> --reason --yes per AC-18.c/e. Wire to KeyRing backing via REST endpoints POST /v1/keys/rotate, POST /v1/keys/{kid}/revoke. Show active keys, rotation window, issued/expires timestamps. `rotate` mints new primary key, marks previous as rotating-out per AC-18.e window.
tasks.notes: Backing comes from KeyRing class (task-004). rotate/revoke are destructive; both require --yes.

tasks.title: Implement rules CLI surface (validate + history)
tasks.description: Create nautilus/cli/rules.py with subcommands: validate <file> (--sandbox, --replay-n N), history --module M (--json) per AC-35.5 + test fixtures. `validate` wraps static.py validator, optionally runs sandbox per --sandbox flag with configurable replay count. `history` lists rule promotion history by module. Wire to validator/static.py and validator/sandbox.py backing.
tasks.notes: Thin CLI wrappers over validator modules. --sandbox integration test harness tested in task-014.

tasks.title: Implement events CLI surface (list event types)
tasks.description: Create nautilus/cli/events.py with single subcommand: list (--json) returning enumeration of all 21 known event_type values per DQ5 (used by drift-guard test in task-001 to ensure Literal vs runtime enumeration stay in sync). Output plain-text list or --json array.
tasks.notes: DQ5 LOCKED: plain-prefix style, no sigils. ~30 LOC per prd.md estimate.

tasks.title: Event-sourcing design spike: decision document + POC
tasks.description: Create design-docs/rkm-event-sourcing-spike.md (≤10 pages per AC-35.1.a) answering: can single append-only event log back both audit and Blackboard-worker persistence? Recommendation: yes/no/hybrid with concrete schema sketch. Create spikes/event-sourcing/ POC replaying ≥10k synthetic events from JSONL into fresh CLIPS env per AC-35.1.b-c. Record storage delta (bytes/event) and replay time at 10k/100k/1M scales. Assert hash-equality of resulting fact set vs known-good snapshot per AC-35.1.b. Decision artifact only; no production wiring per AC-35.1.d.
tasks.notes: Independent task; runs in parallel with all phases. Decision artifact; no integration with v1 broker required. Test POC verifies 10k+ replay at acceptable performance + correctness.
